agent_raw: The evaluation methodology is sound. The use of blocked random windows is justified by two empirical checks conducted before the final comparison: (1) a residual autocorrelation analysis on six months of historical data showing near-zero dependence beyond two hours at the 15-minute forecasting horizon after conditioning on temperature, weekday, holiday status, and lagged load; and (2) a comparison across four historical periods showing less than 1% MAPE difference and no model-order reversals between blocked random and rolling-origin evaluation. The evaluation plan pre-specified blocked random windows based on these empirical findings — this is not post-hoc rationalization. The claim is correctly limited to 15-minute-ahead forecasting with nightly retraining, where the near-i.i.d. residual characterization holds. It explicitly does not extend to seasonal drift or long-range forecasting. The departure from chronological evaluation is justified rather than arbitrary.
